title: Untitled document.pdf
summaries:
* The Himalayas is a mountain range in Asia separating the plains of the Indian subcontinent from the Tibetan Plateau.
* The range contains Earth's highest peaks, including the highest, Mount Everest, and more than 100 peaks exceeding 7,200 metres (23,600 feet) above sea level.
* It spans five countries: Nepal, India, China, Bhutan, and Pakistan, with sovereignty in the Kashmir region disputed among India, Pakistan, and China.
* It is bordered by the Karakoram and Hindu Kush ranges on the northwest, the Tibetan Plateau on the north, and the Indo-Gangetic Plain on the south.
* Major rivers rising in the vicinity of the Himalayas include the Indus, the Ganges, and the Tsangpo–Brahmaputra.
* The combined drainage basin of these rivers is home to about 600 million people, and 53 million people live in the Himalayas.

* The Himalayan drainage basin is home to some 600 million people, and 53 million people live in the Himalayas.
* The mountains have shaped the cultures of South Asia and Tibet, and many peaks are sacred in Hinduism and Buddhism.
* Summits off-limits to climbers include Kangchenjunga (from the Indian side), Gangkhar Puensum, Machapuchare, Nanda Devi, and Kailash.
* The range was uplifted by the collision of the Indian tectonic plate with the Eurasian plate, specifically by folding or nappe-formation of the uppermost Indian crust.
* The Himalayan mountain range runs west-northwest to east-southeast in an arc 2,400 km (1,500 mi) long.
* Its western anchor is Nanga Parba.

* The mantle subducted under Eurasia.
* The Himalayan mountain range runs in a west-northwest to east-southeast arc that is 2,400 km (1,500 mi) long.
* The range's width varies from 350 km (220 mi) in the west to 151 km (94 mi) in the east.
* The western anchor is Nanga Parbat, located south of the northernmost bend of the Indus river, and the eastern anchor is Namcha Barwa, located west of the great bend of the Yarlung Tsangpo River.
* The Indus-Yarlung suture zone separates the Himalayas from the Tibetan plateau.
* The Indus and Yarlung Tsangpo rivers separate the Himalayas from the Karakorams, the Hindu Kush, and the Transhimalaya.
